summary: The corporate register certificate is the base register extract. Under Commercial Act Art. 317(2) it evidences trade name, purpose, head-office/branch address, total capital (자본금의 액), total number and classes of shares, and the names/resident-registration numbers of directors and the representative director. It is publicly obtainable from the court commercial registry (IROS). It proves the company's existence, capital and management — but NOT its shareholders.

evidence.basis: 상법(Commercial Act) 제317조(설립의 등기) 제2항 — registered matters for a stock corporation
evidence.quote: 2. 자본금의 액  3. 발행주식의 총수, 그 종류와 각종주식의 내용과 수 … 8. 사내이사, 사외이사, 그 밖에 상무에 종사하지 아니하는 이사, 감사 및 집행임원의 성명과 주민등록번호  9. 회사를 대표할 이사 또는 집행임원의 성명·주민등록번호 및 주소
evidence.note: Verbatim from statute text (Korean Wikisource transcription of 대한민국 상법). The full enumerated list (items 1–12) contains capital, shares, directors and representative director, but NO item for shareholders/members. Canonical source: law.go.kr 상법 제317조.

evidence.basis: Supreme Court Internet Registry Office (IROS) — the public commercial/corporate register maintained by the courts, from which the 등기사항전부증명서 is issued
evidence.note: The register certificate is the third-party-obtainable extract; its content is fixed by Art. 317, so shareholders never appear on it.

caveats: Modern official name is 등기사항전부증명서 (Certificate of All Registered Matters); 등기부등본 is the legacy term still in common use.
IROS site is session/JS-based and could not be fetched verbatim; the registered-matter list is grounded in the verbatim Art. 317 statute text.

documentLabel: Certified copy of the corporate register / Certificate of All Registered Matters
documentLocal: 법인등기사항전부증명서 (구칭 법인등기부등본) — corporate registration certificate from the court commercial registry (IROS, iros.go.kr)
summary: For a Yuhan Hoesa the corporate register certificate is the base register extract. Under Commercial Act Art. 549 the registered matters include trade name, purpose, head-office address, total capital (자본금의 총액), amount per contribution unit (출자1좌의 금액), and the names/resident-registration numbers of directors and the representative director (and auditor if any). It is publicly obtainable from IROS and proves existence, capital and management — but NOT the members (사원).

evidence.basis: 상법 제549조 (유한회사 설립의 등기) — registered matters for a limited company
evidence.note: Quote omitted — Art. 549 could not be fetched verbatim this session (statute text page truncates before the 유한회사 chapter; law.go.kr JS-rendered). Established content of Art. 549(2): 목적, 상호, 본점 소재지, 자본금의 총액, 출자1좌의 금액, 이사의 성명·주민등록번호, 회사를 대표할 이사의 성명·주소 등 — directors and capital are registered; members are not. Canonical source: law.go.kr 상법 제549조.

evidence.basis: Korean-law reference confirming register content for 유한회사
evidence.quote: 사원의 성명은 등기부에 기재되지 않습니다. … 유한회사는 본점 소재지에서 설립등기를 해야 법인격을 취득합니다.
evidence.note: Secondary confirmation (Korean Wikipedia, 유한회사) that members' names are NOT entered in the register; registration (per Art. 549) establishes legal personality and shows capital/directors.

caveats: Art. 549 not quoted verbatim this session; the enumerated registered matters are from established statute + secondary confirmation, flagged as INFERENCE on exact wording.
Modern name 등기사항전부증명서; 등기부등본 is legacy terminology.

summary: NOT provable from the Registry. Cayman is a secrecy jurisdiction for share ownership: the register of members is kept BY THE COMPANY in writing (s.40), NOT filed with the Registrar. For an exempted company it may be kept anywhere in or outside the Islands (s.44(1)); and where the exempted company does not hold a Cayman business licence its register is NOT open to inspection at all (s.44(2)). The public register (Schedule 1A) records only the ORIGINAL subscribers, not current members. The annual return (s.168) is a mere declaration with no member list. The Registry issues no document evidencing current shareholders.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.40(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall cause to be kept in writing, a register of its members and there shall be entered therein -- (a) the names and addresses of the members of the company, with the addition of, in the case of a company having a capital divided into shares, a statement of the shares held by each member
evidence.note: Register of members is a company-held record, not a Registrar filing.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.44(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The register of members ... shall be kept at the registered office of the company or, in the case of an exempted company, at any other place within or outside the Islands ... Except in the case of an exempted company that does not hold a licence to carry on business in the Islands ... the register of members shall ... be open to the inspection of any member gratis and to the inspection of any other person on payment of ten dollars
evidence.note: Unlicensed exempted companies are carved out — register not open to inspection even by the public; never held at the Registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.168 (annual return of exempted company)
evidence.quote: each exempted company ... shall furnish to the Registrar a return which shall be in the form of a declaration that -- (a) since the previous return or since registration ... there has been no alteration in the memorandum of association
evidence.note: The annual return contains no list of members and no accounts.

caveats: Schedule 1A shows only the subscribers to the memorandum and shares taken at incorporation — founders, not current members.
Beneficial-ownership information is filed (BOTA 2023) but the BO register is NOT public and is distinct from a members list.

summary: NOT provable from the Registry. The register of members is kept BY THE COMPANY in writing (s.40) at its registered office (s.44(1)) and is NOT filed with the Registrar. For an ordinary company it is open to inspection at the registered office (members gratis, others for ten dollars, s.44(2)) — but that is the company's own record, not a registry-issued document. The public register (Schedule 1A) records only original subscribers; no annual filing of current members. The Registry issues no document evidencing current shareholders.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.40(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall cause to be kept in writing, a register of its members and there shall be entered therein -- (a) the names and addresses of the members of the company
evidence.note: 'Every company' — the members register is company-held for ordinary companies too.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.44(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The register of members ... shall be kept at the registered office of the company ... open to the inspection of any member gratis and to the inspection of any other person on payment of ten dollars ... for each inspection
evidence.note: Inspection is at the company's registered office, not the Registry.

caveats: Even though an ordinary company's register of members is inspectable at its registered office, that is a company record, not a registry document — under the KYC 'document = registry proof' rule it is not_provable_from_registry.
Schedule 1A public particulars show only subscribers at incorporation, not current members.

documentLabel: Certified copy of Memorandum and Articles of Association
summary: The constitution (Memorandum and Articles of Association) is filed with the Registrar at incorporation: they 'shall be delivered in duplicate to the Registrar who shall file and retain the original' (s.26(1)). The Registry holds the original and issues certified copies — the base document for the constitution. Secrecy caveat: for an exempted company the M&A itself is NOT open to general public inspection (s.26(3A) opens only the Schedule 1A register particulars); certified copies are obtained by or with the company's authority.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of association and the articles of association, if any, shall be delivered in duplicate to the Registrar who shall file and retain the original thereof and shall return the duplicate thereof endorsed with a memorandum of registration
evidence.note: M&A filed and retained by the Registrar; certified copies obtainable.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(3A)
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall make the register under subsection (3) available for inspection by any person on payment of the fee specified in Part 1A of Schedule 5
evidence.note: Public inspection is limited to the Schedule 1A register particulars — NOT the M&A document itself.

caveats: Document is definitively filed/retained by the Registrar, but public access is restricted — certified copies are typically obtained by or through the company.
The register holds the AS-FILED M&A plus recorded alterations; confirm currency via the company's consolidated M&A.

documentLabel: Certified copy of Memorandum and Articles of Association
summary: The constitution is delivered to and retained by the Registrar at incorporation (s.26(1)), and the Registry issues certified copies — a registry-evidenced base document. For an ordinary (resident) company the statute opens only the Schedule 1A register particulars to inspection by any person (s.26(3A)); certified copies of the M&A are the reliable proof.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of association and the articles of association, if any, shall be delivered in duplicate to the Registrar who shall file and retain the original thereof
evidence.note: Applies to all companies registered under the Act, including ordinary companies.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.29
evidence.quote: A copy of the memorandum of association having annexed thereto the articles of association
evidence.note: Company must give members a copy; a certified Registrar copy is the KYC-grade proof.

caveats: UNVERIFIED whether an ordinary company's M&A is freely inspectable by the general public at the Registry; the statute expressly opens only the Schedule 1A register particulars (s.26(3A)). Certified copies remain the dependable route.
Confirm currency: the register holds the as-filed M&A plus recorded alterations.

summary: Cayman does NOT issue a comprehensive itemised current register extract. The Registrar keeps a public register (s.26(3), Schedule 1A) recording only incorporation particulars (name, registered-office location, capital/shares, ORIGINAL subscribers, dates, company number, nature of business, FY-end) — not current shareholders. The registry-issued current-status document is the Certificate of Good Standing (s.200A): evidence that the company is in good standing on the date of issue. Base document; proves existence/good standing only, not ownership, capital or management.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.200A(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The Registrar may on application made by a company issue a certificate of good standing to a company that is in good standing in accordance with subsection (2). A certificate of good standing is evidence of the fact that the company is in good standing on the date that the certificate of good standing is issued.
evidence.note: Registry-issued status snapshot, not an itemised extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(3A) / Schedule 1A
evidence.quote: The Registrar shall make the register under subsection (3) available for inspection by any person on payment of the fee specified in Part 1A of Schedule 5
evidence.note: Schedule 1A limits the public register to name, registered-office location, capital/shares, subscribers, incorporation dates, number, nature of business, FY-end — not current members or directors.

caveats: INFERENCE: mapping the Certificate of Good Standing to 'Registerauszug' is an analogue — Cayman issues no full itemised current extract; the CoGS proves standing only.

Since the 2023 Beneficial Ownership Transparency amendments the Registrar also maintains a publicly inspectable list of the NAMES of current directors (s.55A), on payment — a separate inspection, not a certified extract. Shareholders remain non-public.

summary: Same regime as for exempted companies. The public register (s.26(3), Schedule 1A) records only incorporation particulars and original subscribers — not current members. The registry-issued current-status document is the Certificate of Good Standing (s.200A). No comprehensive current register extract exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.200A(1)-(2)
evidence.quote: The Registrar may on application made by a company issue a certificate of good standing to a company that is in good standing in accordance with subsection (2).
evidence.note: s.200A applies to companies generally (Part 11), not only exempted companies.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (2025 Revision) (Cayman) s.26(1),(3),(3A) / Schedule 1A
evidence.quote: A register of companies shall be kept by the Registrar in which shall be entered the particulars set out in Schedule 1A
evidence.note: Public register holds Schedule 1A particulars only.

caveats: INFERENCE: Certificate of Good Standing mapped to 'Registerauszug' as the nearest registry-issued current document; it does not itemise members, directors or capital.
Since 2023 the Registrar maintains a publicly inspectable list of the NAMES of current directors (s.55A), on payment.

summary: Shareholders (Aktionäre) of a Liechtenstein AG are NOT entered in the Handelsregister and do not appear on the extract. Namenaktien are recorded only in a company-internal Aktienbuch; Inhaberaktien are deposited with a Verwahrer who keeps a private register. Neither is public. The register lists only organs — never the owners.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft, Art. 328 Abs. 2 PGR
evidence.quote: Die Gesellschaft hat über die Eigentümer der Namenaktien (Aktionäre) ein Verzeichnis (Aktienbuch) mit den gesetzlich vorgeschriebenen Angaben zu führen.
evidence.note: Aktienbuch is kept BY the company, not filed with or held by the Handelsregister.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft, Art. 326a/326c PGR (Inhaberaktien)
evidence.quote: Inhaberaktien sind beim Verwahrer zu hinterlegen. Dieser hat ein Register zu führen, in welchem sämtliche gesetzlich vorgesehenen Angaben über den Inhaberaktionär zu registrieren sind.
evidence.note: Verwahrer register is private (accessible to authorities), not the public Handelsregister.

caveats: Liechtenstein is privacy-oriented: AG shareholders are structurally non-public. Ownership reachable only via the non-public beneficial-ownership register (VwbPG) for authorities/obliged entities — never from the commercial register.

summary: An Anstalt has no shareholders; ownership/control sits with the holder(s) of the founder's rights (Inhaber der Gründerrechte) and, economically, the beneficiaries (Destinatäre). Neither is disclosed on the Handelsregister extract — founder rights are commonly held via a trustee, and beneficiary provisions typically sit in non-public Beistatuten. There is no register document evidencing the owners.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Anstalt (organisation)
evidence.quote: Bei einer Anstalt mit Gründerrechten ist in der Regel der oder die Inhaber der Gründerrechte oberstes Organ.
evidence.note: The Merkblatt lists the entities that ARE registered (Verwaltung, Revisionsstelle, Repräsentanz) but does not list the Inhaber der Gründerrechte as register content.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Anstalt, Art. 536 Abs. 2 PGR (Begünstigte)
evidence.quote: Die Statuten oder Beistatuten haben zudem auch nähere Bestimmungen über allfällige Begünstigte (Destinatäre) zu enthalten. Bei Fehlen einer solchen Regelung gilt die gesetzliche Vermutung, dass der Inhaber der Gründerrechte selbst Begünstigter ist.
evidence.note: Beneficiary provisions are placed in Statuten/Beistatuten; Beistatuten are not part of the publicly accessible register content.

caveats: INFERENCE: the Merkblatt does not explicitly say 'founder-rights holders are NOT registered', but they are absent from the enumerated register content and Liechtenstein practice holds them via trustees; owners are therefore not evidenced by any register document.
Beneficial ownership reachable only via the non-public VwbPG register.

summary: KEY POINT: unlike an AG, the members (Gesellschafter) of a Liechtenstein GmbH ARE entered in the Handelsregister together with their Stammeinlagen and therefore appear on the register extract — parallel to Austria/Switzerland. No separate filed Gesellschafterliste is needed; the register extract itself is the proof of current members. The internal Anteilbuch (share ledger) is a company-kept book, not the public proof.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur GmbH, Art. 394 Abs. 1 PGR
evidence.quote: Die Gesellschafter werden samt den gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und ihren Stammeinlagen im Handelsregister eingetragen.
evidence.note: The decisive provision: current members are register content, hence on the Auszug.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur GmbH, Art. 402 Abs. 1 PGR (Anteilbuch)
evidence.quote: Über die Stammeinlagen aller Gesellschafter ist ein Anteilbuch zu führen, aus dem die gesetzlichen Angaben zu den Gesellschaftern, der Betrag der übernommenen Einlage sowie jeder Übergang einer Stammeinlage und jede Änderung ersichtlich ist.
evidence.note: The Anteilbuch is an internal book kept by the company — NOT a publicly filed Gesellschafterliste; the public proof of members is the register entry/extract.

caveats: Beneficial ownership (>25%) beyond the registered legal members is held in the separate non-public beneficial-ownership register (VwbPG), not the commercial register.
The register shows legal members and their Stammeinlagen; it does not show economic/fiduciary arrangements behind a registered member.

summary: A Stiftung has no shareholders/members; the relevant persons are the founder (Stifter) and the beneficiaries (Begünstigte). These are NOT public. Beneficiaries are defined in the Stiftungsurkunde or a Stiftungszusatzurkunde (Beistatut), which for private-benefit foundations are not publicly accessible; the founder is likewise not disclosed via the register. No register document evidences owners/beneficiaries.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Stiftung, Art. 552 §16 Abs. 1 Ziff. 4 PGR (Begünstigte)
evidence.quote: Bei der privatnützigen Stiftung muss der in der Stiftungsurkunde durch den Stifter festzulegende Zweck auch die Bezeichnung der konkreten oder nach objektiven Merkmalen individualisierbaren Begünstigten oder des Begünstigtenkreises umfassen, sofern nicht stattdessen ausdrücklich auf eine Stiftungszusatzurkunde verwiesen wird, welche dies regelt.
evidence.note: Beneficiaries sit in the Stiftungsurkunde/Beistatut, not part of the publicly accessible register for private-benefit foundations.

caveats: Liechtenstein privacy core: Stifter and Begünstigte are never public. Reachable, if at all, only via the non-public VwbPG beneficial-ownership register for authorities/obliged entities.

summary: The AG's Statuten are a mandatory formation document filed with the Handelsregister. Not printed on the extract, but retrievable from the public register file (Art. 953 PGR public-access regime for AG).

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft, Art. 279 PGR
evidence.quote: Die Statuten der Aktiengesellschaft müssen die gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und Bestimmungen enthalten

evidence.basis: Art. 953 PGR (Einsicht in den Handelsregisterakt) — reported via de.Wikipedia 'Handelsregister (Liechtenstein)'
evidence.note: Secondary source: full public inspection of the register file (incl. filed Statuten) applies to AG since 1 Jan 2017; not independently verified against statute text.

caveats: UNVERIFIED against primary statute text for the access-scope claim (secondary source).
Retrieval via Amt für Justiz on request, with fee.

summary: The Anstalt is formed by written Statuten signed by all founders and filed with the Handelsregister; the Statuten are retrievable from the public register file (Art. 953 public-access regime covers Anstalten). Beistatuten (which may name beneficiaries) are NOT publicly accessible.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Anstalt, Art. 536 Abs. 2 PGR
evidence.quote: Zur Gründung der Anstalt bedarf es schriftlicher und von sämtlichen Gründern unterzeichneter Statuten.

evidence.basis: Art. 953 PGR public-access regime — reported via de.Wikipedia 'Handelsregister (Liechtenstein)'
evidence.note: Secondary source indicates full register-file access applies to Anstalten (unlike Stiftungen/Treuhänderschaften); not independently verified.

caveats: UNVERIFIED access-scope (secondary source).
Only the Statuten are within scope; Beistatuten/beneficiary regulations remain private.

summary: The GmbH's Statuten (Gesellschaftsvertrag) are filed with the Handelsregister at formation. They are a supporting document (Beleg) in the Handelsregisterakt, not printed on the extract, but retrievable: since 1 Jan 2017 the register file is public for GmbH/AG/Anstalt without proof of interest (Art. 953 PGR).

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur GmbH, Art. 390 Abs. 2 PGR
evidence.quote: Die Statuten (Gesellschaftsvertrag) der GmbH müssen die gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und Bestimmungen enthalten.
evidence.note: Statuten are a mandatory formation document filed with the register.

evidence.basis: Art. 953 PGR (Öffentlichkeit / Einsicht in den Handelsregisterakt) — reported via de.Wikipedia 'Handelsregister (Liechtenstein)'
evidence.note: Secondary source: since 1 Jan 2017 anyone may inspect the Handelsregisterakt (including filed Belege such as Statuten) for all registered legal forms without demonstrating a legitimate interest; restriction applies only to Stiftungen and Treuhänderschaften. Not independently verified against the statute text.

caveats: UNVERIFIED against primary statute text: the Art. 953 PGR public-access scope is taken from a secondary (Wikipedia) source; the Merkblatt confirms Statuten are filed but does not state the access regime.
In practice document retrieval requires ordering from the Amt für Justiz and a fee.

summary: The foundation's constitution is the Stiftungsurkunde (Statut). It is a mandatory document, but for the typical (private-benefit/deposited) foundation it is subject to the restricted-public principle and is NOT freely retrievable from the register — unlike GmbH/AG/Anstalt Statuten. Treat as not obtainable as a public document by default.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Stiftung, Art. 552 §16 Abs. 1 PGR
evidence.quote: Die Stiftungsurkunde (Statut) der Stiftung muss die gesetzlich erforderlichen Angaben und Bestimmungen enthalten.
evidence.note: Document exists and is filed/deposited, but the access regime for foundations is restricted.

evidence.basis: Restricted register publicity for Stiftungen/Treuhänderschaften (Art. 953 PGR carve-out) — reported via de.Wikipedia 'Handelsregister (Liechtenstein)'
evidence.note: Secondary source: foundations and trust enterprises are excluded from full public register-file inspection. Not independently verified against statute text.

caveats: CONFLICT by sub-type: for a registered gemeinnützige (charitable) foundation the Stiftungsurkunde may be obtainable; for the common privatnützige/family foundation it is not. Default status set to not_provable_from_registry to reflect the KYC-relevant majority case.
UNVERIFIED access-scope against primary statute (secondary source).

summary: The AG must be registered in the Handelsregister and acquires legal personality only upon registration; the extract evidences firm, seat, capital, and the registered organs (Verwaltungsrat, Revisionsstelle, any Aufsichtsrat, Verwahrer, Repräsentanz).

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Aktiengesellschaft (Amt für Justiz, AJU/h70.004.07, 07/2025), Art. 261 ff. PGR
evidence.quote: Die Aktiengesellschaft muss zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden.

caveats: Register is public for AG; extracts issued in certified form.

summary: The Anstalt (establishment) must be registered in the Handelsregister and acquires legal personality only on registration; an extract exists showing firm, seat, capital and the Verwaltung/Repräsentanz.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Anstalt (Amt für Justiz, AJU/h70.005.09, 06/2025), Art. 534 ff. PGR
evidence.quote: Die Anstalt muss zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden.

caveats: Extract shows the administrative organ (Verwaltung), not the economic controllers.

summary: The GmbH must be entered in the Liechtenstein Handelsregister and acquires legal personality only upon registration; the current register extract (Handelsregisterauszug) is the base document evidencing existence, firm, seat, capital, management (Geschäftsführer) and representation.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur GmbH (Amt für Justiz, AJU/h70.007.09, 07/2025), Art. 389 f. PGR
evidence.quote: Die GmbH muss zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden.
evidence.note: Rechtspersönlichkeit mit Eintragung (Art. 390 Abs. 1 PGR).

caveats: Certified extracts (beglaubigt) are the standard product; the register (handelsregister.li / oera.li) is public for GmbH.

summary: Split regime: gemeinnützige (charitable) foundations MUST be registered and have a register extract; privatnützige (private-benefit, e.g. family) foundations are usually only DEPOSITED (Gründungsanzeige hinterlegt) and are not entered in the register — for those there is no substantive public extract. Even for registered foundations the 'restricted public principle' limits public access.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Stiftung (Amt für Justiz, AJU/h70.008.10), Art. 552 §14 PGR
evidence.quote: Gemeinnützige Stiftungen müssen zwingend im Handelsregister eingetragen werden; privatnützige Stiftungen können sich freiwillig im Handelsregister eintragen lassen, hingegen müssen sie im Handelsregister eingetragen werden, wenn sie auf spezialgesetzlicher Grundlage ein nach kaufmännischer Art geführtes Gewerbe betreiben.

evidence.basis: Merkblatt zur Stiftung, Art. 552 §20 Abs. 1 PGR (Hinterlegung)
evidence.quote: Unterliegt die Stiftung keiner Eintragungspflicht, ist binnen 30 Tagen ab Errichtung der Stiftung eine Gründungsanzeige beim Amt für Justiz zu hinterlegen.
evidence.note: Deposited foundations are not 'eingetragen' — only a Gründungsanzeige is filed, so no ordinary public register extract with substantive content exists.

caveats: CONFLICT by sub-type: only registered (mostly gemeinnützige) foundations yield a real extract; the typical privatnützige family foundation is merely deposited (hinterlegt) with restricted public access.
Register access for Stiftungen is governed by the restricted-public principle.

summary: Shareholders are not filed. The share register is kept at the registered office.

evidence.basis: Guichet.lu — société anonyme
evidence.quote: Un registre des actions nominatives qui établit la propriété est tenu au siège social.

caveats: UNVERIFIED: whether an SA's sole shareholder must be disclosed (EU 12th Directive / §42 AktG analogue). No source found. Treat as unclear.

summary: Members are mandatory public RCS content and appear in the extract itself. Kept current: share transfers must be filed and published.

evidence.basis: Loi du 19 décembre 2002 (RCS)
evidence.quote: l'identité des associés, leur adresse privée ou professionnelle précise et le nombre de parts sociales détenues par chacun

evidence.basis: Cession de parts — RCS/RESA publication
evidence.note: Share transfers must be notarised or by private deed, then filed with the RCS and published via RESA, so the associés data stays current. Contrasted explicitly with the SA, where transfers are not visible to third parties.

caveats: Delivered as extract content, not as a discrete list document.

documentLocal: annual return (Seventh Schedule, Part 3 — List of Past and Present Members)
summary: CONFIRMED: Malta's annual return IS a full shareholder list and IS public. Art.184 requires every company, on each anniversary of registration, to file an annual return in the form of the Seventh Schedule. Part 3 is the 'List of Past and Present Members' giving each member's name, address and number of shares held. It is a document kept by the Registrar and thus inspectable/obtainable by any person under art.424. The founding shareholders are additionally named as subscribers in the memorandum (art.69). A separately-ordered document, not part of the base extract, hence 'additional'.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 184(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall, after 1st January 2004, upon each anniversary of its registration, make a return in the form set out in the Seventh Schedule showing the matters therein specified and made up to the date of such anniversary

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), Seventh Schedule, Part 3 (Article 184)
evidence.quote: 3. List of Past and Present Members. List of persons holding shares or stock in the company on the date to which this return is made up, and of persons who have held shares or stock therein at any time since the date of the last return, or in the case of the first return, of the registration of the company.
evidence.note: Columns: Names / Addresses / Number of shares held by existing members.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424 (public access)
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain ... a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal (official) — documents obtainable from MBR
evidence.quote: This documentation includes, inter alia, all the notifications submitted and registered by the Registrar for every company, the status of the company, annual accounts as well as annual returns.

caveats: STALENESS: the annual return is filed only once a year (within 42 days of the registration anniversary), so at any given moment it may be up to ~13 months out of date and will not reflect share transfers since the last filing.

The company-held register of members (art.123) is the live record but is kept at the registered office and open to inspection only by members without charge (art.125) — NOT a Registrar/MBR document. The public MBR proof is the annual return.

Do not confuse with the beneficial-ownership register: BO is a separate filing whose public access was curtailed after the CJEU Nov-2022 ruling; the annual-return member list is unaffected and remains public.

documentLocal: annual return (Seventh Schedule, Part 3 — List of Past and Present Members)
summary: The art.184 annual-return obligation and the Seventh Schedule Part 3 'List of Past and Present Members' apply to ALL companies, with no exemption for public companies (the only carve-out from Parts 2 and 3 is for investment companies with variable share capital / SICAVs, art.84(8)). So a plc's annual return also legally lists every member and their shareholding, and it is public under art.424. Founding subscribers are additionally in the memorandum (art.69).

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 184(1)
evidence.quote: Every company shall, after 1st January 2004, upon each anniversary of its registration, make a return in the form set out in the Seventh Schedule showing the matters therein specified and made up to the date of such anniversary

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 84(8) — scope of exemption (only SICAVs excused)
evidence.quote: an investment company with variable share capital shall not be obliged to give any of the details in Parts 2 and 3 of the form of annual return set out in the Seventh Schedule, other than the share capital of the company and the number of shares issued.
evidence.note: Confirms a plain plc has NO such exemption — it must list members.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424 (public access)
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ...

caveats: STALENESS: same annual, up-to-~13-months lag as the Ltd — the list IS complete but is a yearly snapshot.

NOMINEE/LISTED RISK: for a plc whose shares are admitted to trading, the registered members shown in the annual return are frequently nominees, custodians or a CSD, not the ultimate beneficial holders, and registered holdings turn over continuously; the document proves the register of members, not beneficial ownership.
BO-register public-access restriction (post-CJEU 2022) does not affect the annual-return member list.

summary: A Maltese company's constitution is its memorandum of association (art.69) plus its articles of association (art.75). Both are delivered to and registered by the Registrar (art.76) and held as the company's 'statute' at the MBR; any person may obtain a certified copy under art.424. Treated as an additional (separately-ordered) document rather than the base extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 69(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of every company shall state: (a) whether the company is a public company or a private company; (b) the name and residence of each of the subscribers thereto; (c) the name of the company; (d) the registered office in Malta ...; (e) the objects of the company; (f) the amount of share capital ...

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 75(1)
evidence.quote: There may be registered with the memorandum, articles of association (hereinafter referred to as "articles") which shall be signed by the subscribers to the memorandum and prescribing regulations for the company.
evidence.note: If no articles are registered, the model articles in the First Schedule apply (art.75(2)).

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 76(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum and articles, if any, shall be delivered for registration to the Registrar who, being satisfied that all the requirements ... have been complied with, shall register them.

caveats: A private company may register bespoke articles OR rely on the First Schedule model articles (art.75(2)).
The MBR-held copy reflects the memorandum and articles as last amended and registered; unregistered/pending amendments would not appear.

summary: Same constitutional framework as the Ltd: memorandum (art.69, stating it is a public company) and articles (art.75), both registered with the Registrar (art.76) and obtainable as certified copies by any person (art.424). A public company must additionally annex a formation-costs/special-advantages statement (art.69(2)), likewise a registered document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 69(1)(a) and 69(2)
evidence.quote: The memorandum of every company shall state: (a) whether the company is a public company or a private company ... (2) In the case of a public company, there shall be annexed to the memorandum a document providing: (a) the total amount or an estimate of all the costs payable by the company or chargeable to it by reason of its formation ... and (b) a description of any special advantage granted prior to the time the company is authorized to commence business ...

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 76(1)
evidence.quote: The memorandum and articles, if any, shall be delivered for registration to the Registrar who, being satisfied that all the requirements ... have been complied with, shall register them.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424 (public access)
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain ... a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

caveats: The MBR copy reflects the constitution as last registered; any unregistered amendment would not be evidenced.
Model-articles fallback (art.75(2)) applies as for any company where articles are not separately registered.

summary: The Malta Business Registry (MBR) holds the statutory register and issues a certified company extract and a certificate of registration. Under Companies Act art.424 any person may inspect documents kept by the Registrar and obtain a certified copy or extract. Core identifying data (name, number, legal form, registered office, incorporation date, status, share capital, officers) is publicly available; the extract is the functional equivalent of a German Registerauszug.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain a copy of the certificate of the registration of any commercial partnership or a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.
evidence.note: General public-access provision covering all Registrar-held documents.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 77(2)
evidence.quote: A certificate of registration given in respect of a company is conclusive evidence that the requirements of this Act in respect of registration and of matters precedent and incidental to it have been complied with and that the company is duly registered as a public or private company under this Act.

evidence.basis: EU e-Justice Portal (official) — Malta business register
evidence.quote: company names and registration numbers, their registered address, date of incorporation, whether it is a company or not, share capital, and the identity of the company officials.
evidence.note: Free public information tier; further documents available for a charge.

caveats: INFERENCE: Malta has no statutory document literally called a 'Registerauszug'; the MBR-issued company extract / certificate of registration is the functional equivalent.
The free online extract is a data snapshot; a certified extract must be ordered and carries a fee.

summary: Identical basis to the Ltd: art.424 makes all Registrar-held documents inspectable and obtainable as certified copies/extracts, and art.77(2) makes the certificate of registration conclusive evidence of due registration as a public company. The MBR issues a company extract that functions as the register extract.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 424
evidence.quote: Any person may - (a) inspect the documents kept by the Registrar ... (b) obtain a copy of the certificate of the registration of any commercial partnership or a copy or extract of any other document, and require it to be certified by the Registrar as a true copy.

evidence.basis: Companies Act (Cap. 386), art. 77(2)
evidence.quote: A certificate of registration given in respect of a company is conclusive evidence that the requirements of this Act in respect of registration and of matters precedent and incidental to it have been complied with and that the company is duly registered as a public or private company under this Act.

caveats: INFERENCE: same mapping caveat as the Ltd — 'Registerauszug' is our label for the MBR company extract.

documentLocal: company search / register extract; annual return (Tenth Schedule)
summary: Current shareholders of a domestic company are registry-held and publicly obtainable: shareholder particulars are lodged at incorporation (s.23(2)(d)) and the annual return filed with the Registrar must list every shareholder's name/address and shares held (s.223 + Tenth Schedule para 11). Mauritius is comparatively transparent and public register access under s.14 covers these particulars, so shareholders are proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) Tenth Schedule (Sections 223(5) and 269(2)), para 11
evidence.quote: the following information relating to past and present shareholders of the company-- (a) the names and addresses (other than residential) of all the shareholders of the company; ... (c) the number of shares held by each shareholder;
evidence.note: Annual return filed with the Registrar carries the full current shareholder list; registry-held.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.91(1),(3)
evidence.quote: A company shall maintain a share register ... The share register ... shall state ... (a)(i) the names, in alphabetical order, and the last known address of each person who is, or has within the last 7 years been, a shareholder; ... (b) the number of shares of that class held by each shareholder
evidence.note: The statutory share register is COMPANY-kept, not filed; the registry evidences shareholders via the annual return and incorporation particulars.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.23(2)(d) (application for incorporation)
evidence.quote: the full name, the usual residential address and the service address of every shareholder of the proposed company, and the number of shares to be issued to every shareholder
evidence.note: Initial shareholders lodged with the Registrar at incorporation.

caveats: The authoritative statutory share register is company-held (s.91), not a registry document; the registry proves current shareholders via the annual return (may lag up to ~1 year) plus incorporation particulars.

UNVERIFIED whether the live CBRD/CBRIS register extract itemizes CURRENT shareholders on its face; if not, the correct status is proved_by_additional_document (the latest annual return). Confidence medium.

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Global Business Licence (Global Business Company)
summary: Although a GBC files shareholder particulars with the CBRD (incorporation particulars and annual return, Tenth Schedule para 11), those documents are not disclosable to the public: s.14(7) restricts access to shareholders/officers/management company/registered agent, and s.14(8) limits third-party disclosure to name, registered office and registered agent. Current shareholders of a GBC cannot be proved from the registry by an outside KYC party.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(7)
evidence.quote: This section shall not apply to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company unless the person is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent of that company.
evidence.note: Shareholder particulars on the register/annual return are not publicly obtainable for a GBC.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) Tenth Schedule para 11 (via s.223(5))
evidence.quote: the following information relating to past and present shareholders of the company-- (a) the names and addresses (other than residential) of all the shareholders of the company; ... (c) the number of shares held by each shareholder;
evidence.note: GBCs file this annual return too, but s.14(7) makes it non-public.

caveats: The LEGAL shareholder list IS filed with CBRD; it is withheld from third parties by s.14(7) — an insider can obtain it.
Beneficial owners of a GBC sit with the FSC and the licensed management company; GBC shares are frequently held via nominees, so any registered shareholder may itself be a nominee.
Public-facing disclosure is capped at name + registered office + management company/registered agent (s.14(8)).

summary: Where a company has a constitution, a certified copy is lodged with the incorporation application (s.23(1)(c)(vii)) and becomes a registered document obtainable from the Registrar under s.14. It is a separate/additional document from the register extract. However, a private company need not have a constitution: absent one it is governed by the model constitution in the Second Schedule (s.42(1)(b)), in which case no bespoke registry document exists.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.23(1)(c)(vii)
evidence.quote: where the proposed company is to have a constitution, a document certified by at least one applicant that the document is the company's constitution.
evidence.note: Constitution (if any) is lodged at incorporation and obtainable as a copy/extract under s.14(2)(b).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.42(1)(b)
evidence.quote: in the case of a private company incorporated under Part III, be, subject to section 40, the constitution set out in the Second Schedule;
evidence.note: A private company with no adopted constitution runs on the statutory model constitution — no company-specific document filed.

caveats: Conditional existence: if the company adopted no constitution (default Second Schedule model), there is NO registry document — for such companies this kind is effectively not_provable_from_registry.
Constitution is an additional document to the register extract, obtainable on request/payment (s.14).

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Global Business Licence (Global Business Company)
summary: A GBC's constitution is lodged with the CBRD at incorporation (s.23(1)(c)(vii)) and GBCs almost always adopt a bespoke constitution, but as a registered document it falls under the s.14(7) restriction: only a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent may obtain copies/extracts. It is not obtainable from the registry by an outside KYC party (s.14(8)).

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(7)
evidence.quote: This section shall not apply to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company unless the person is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent of that company.
evidence.note: The GBC's registered constitution is not publicly obtainable.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.23(1)(c)(vii) and s.42(1)(a)
evidence.quote: where the proposed company is to have a constitution, a document certified by at least one applicant that the document is the company's constitution.
evidence.note: Constitution is lodged at incorporation (GBCs typically have one), but disclosure is blocked by s.14(7).

caveats: The constitution EXISTS at the registry for most GBCs but is withheld from third parties (s.14(7)); an insider can obtain it.
Assumes GBC is a private company (standard case).

summary: The Registrar of Companies (CBRD) keeps the company register and, on payment of the prescribed fee, issues a copy of or certified extract from the registered documents; the CBRD also runs a free online company search (CBRIS). For an ordinary domestic company this register extract is publicly obtainable and is the base document.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(2)(b) and s.14(3)
evidence.quote: A person may ... apply to the Registrar for-- ... (b) a copy of, or extract from, a document in a register kept by the Registrar ... On an application under subsection (2), the Registrar shall, on payment by the applicant of the prescribed fee, issue the document, particulars or copy or certified copy applied for.
evidence.note: General public right to obtain an extract from the register for a domestic company.

evidence.basis: CBRD official site (companies.govmu.org) — online services
evidence.quote: Search Online (free of Charge)
evidence.note: Free CBRIS online company search; certified extracts obtainable via 'Request of Documents'.

caveats: The free online CBRIS search returns data on screen; the actual DOCUMENT for KYC is the certified extract issued by the Registrar on payment (s.14(3)).
INFERENCE: exact label/format of the CBRD register extract not verified from a live extract (search UI is a JS app); classification by statute, confidence high.

legalFormLocal: Company holding a Global Business Licence (Global Business Company)
summary: A GBC is incorporated/registered under the Companies Act 2001 and is on the CBRD register, but s.14(7) disapplies the public inspection-and-copies regime to a private company holding a Global Business Licence unless the requester is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent. To the general public (KYC third party) the Registrar discloses only company name, registered office, and the management company/registered agent (s.14(8)). A full register extract is not obtainable from the registry by an outside party.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(7)
evidence.quote: This section shall not apply to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company unless the person is a shareholder, officer, management company or registered agent of that company.
evidence.note: Removes the general public right to inspect/obtain copies or extracts of a GBC's registered documents.

evidence.basis: Companies Act 2001 (Mauritius) s.14(8)
evidence.quote: a person may, on payment of the prescribed fee, request the Registrar to provide, in relation to a private company holding a Global Business Licence or an Authorised Company-- (a) the name of the company and the address of its registered office; and (b) the name and address of any management company or registered agent appointed by the company
evidence.note: Defines the ONLY registry data a third party can obtain for a GBC — no full extract.

caveats: GBC IS on the CBRD register (a company under Companies Act 2001 holding a Global Business Licence from the FSC); the block is on PUBLIC disclosure, not on registration.
An insider (shareholder/officer/management company/registered agent) CAN obtain the extract; beneficial-ownership data sits with the FSC and the licensed management company.
Assumes the GBC is a private company (near-universal case).

legalFormLocal: Sociedad de Responsabilidad Limitada de Capital Variable
documentLabel: Partners and their parts sociales as stated in the constitutive deed (and registered amendments)
documentLocal: Acta constitutiva / escritura pública (socios y partes sociales), más escrituras de cesión/modificación inscritas en el RPC; el libro especial de socios (art. 73 LGSM) lo lleva la sociedad, no el registro
summary: Unlike the S.A., the partners (socios) and their parts sociales are a mandatory content of the escritura constitutiva (LGSM art. 6), which is a base registered instrument, so the founding membership is provable from the registry. Admission or transfer of partners requires partner consent and is typically effected by a notarised amendment that is inscribed in the RPC, so changes can be evidenced by registered additional deeds. However, the authoritative current membership is the company-held libro especial de socios (art. 73), which is kept 'al cuidado de los administradores' and is not a registry document, so the registry cannot guarantee a fully current list on its own.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 6
evidence.quote: Los nombres, nacionalidad y domicilio de las personas físicas o morales que constituyan la sociedad
evidence.note: Founding socios are stated in the registered escritura constitutiva.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 73
evidence.quote: La sociedad llevará un libro especial de los socios, en el cual se inscribirá el nombre y el domicilio de cada uno, con indicación de sus aportaciones, y la transmisión de las partes sociales.
evidence.note: The definitive member list is a company-held book, kept 'al cuidado de los administradores', not a registry filing.

caveats: The base acta proves only founding membership; a current list depends on whether each transfer/admission was notarised and inscribed (registered additional documents).
The authoritative current membership register (libro especial de socios, art. 73) is company-held and off-registry, so the registry alone may not yield a fully current, canonical Gesellschafterliste.
INFERENCE: routine inscription of every partner transfer in practice is asserted, not confirmed by a fetched quote; some transfers may go unregistered.

summary: For a sociedad anónima the shareholders are recorded only in the company-held libro de registro de acciones (LGSM art. 128), not in the public registry; the company treats as owner whoever appears in that book and records transfers on request (art. 129). Only the founding shareholders named in the acta constitutiva reach the RPC. Therefore a current shareholder list cannot be proved from the registry.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 128
evidence.quote: Las sociedades anónimas tendrán un registro de acciones que contendrá: I.- El nombre, la nacionalidad y el domicilio del accionista, y la indicación de las acciones que le pertenezcan
evidence.note: The share register is a company book, not a registry filing.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 129
evidence.quote: La sociedad considerará como dueño de las acciones a quien aparezca inscrito como tal en el registro...A este efecto, la sociedad deberá inscribir en dicho registro, a petición de cualquier titular, las transmisiones que se efectúen.
evidence.note: Ownership and transfers are tracked in the company-held book, off-registry.

caveats: The founding shareholders do appear in the acta constitutiva (registered), but that is a snapshot at incorporation, not a maintained current shareholder list.
The authoritative current list (libro de registro de acciones) is held by the company and is not a public-registry document.

summary: The constitution is the escritura constitutiva (acta constitutiva) with the estatutos sociales, granted before a notary and inscribed in the RPC (LGSM arts. 2, 6, 7). It is the base registered instrument and its certified copy is the registry's core proof of the company's charter.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 6
evidence.quote: El importe del capital social.
evidence.note: The escritura constitutiva embeds the estatutos and required capital clauses.

evidence.note: Constitution granted in escritura pública before a notary and then inscribed.

documentLocal: Acta constitutiva / escritura pública con estatutos sociales (copia certificada), inscrita en el Registro Público de Comercio
summary: The constitution is the escritura constitutiva (acta constitutiva) containing the estatutos sociales, granted before a notary and inscribed in the RPC (LGSM arts. 5-7). evidence.quote: Los nombres, nacionalidad y domicilio de las personas físicas o morales que constituyan la sociedad
evidence.note: Escritura constitutiva must state founders and capital; it embeds the estatutos.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 7

summary: As with any mercantile company, the S. de R.L. de C.V. acquires legal personality by inscription of its escritura constitutiva in the Registro Público de Comercio (LGSM art. 2) on an electronic mercantile folio in SIGER. The registry evidences registered particulars via the certification/boleta of that folio. Mexico issues no consolidated German-style current-particulars extract; the folio certifies inscribed instruments.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 2
evidence.quote: Las sociedades mercantiles inscritas en el Registro Público de Comercio, tienen personalidad jurídica distinta de la de los socios.
evidence.note: Inscription in the RPC is the constitutive registry act for all mercantile forms.

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio arts. 20-32 (folio mercantil electrónico / SIGER)
evidence.note: UNVERIFIED verbatim — Código de Comercio text could not be fetched; folio/SIGER/boleta model asserted from domain knowledge.

caveats: The RPC certifies inscriptions on the folio mercantil; it does not synthesise a German-style consolidated current extract.
Capital-variable changes are often not re-registered, so the folio may lag actual capital.
INFERENCE: exact deliverable name (certificación de folio / boleta) not confirmed by a fetched verbatim quote.

documentLabel: Commercial register extract — certification of the electronic mercantile folio
documentLocal: Certificación / boleta de inscripción del folio mercantil electrónico (Registro Público de Comercio, vía SIGER)
summary: A mercantile company acquires legal personality by inscription of its escritura constitutiva in the Registro Público de Comercio (LGSM art. 2), kept as an electronic mercantile folio (folio mercantil electrónico) in SIGER. The registry evidences the registered particulars through a certification/boleta of that folio, which is the closest analogue to a German Registerauszug. Note that Mexico issues no consolidated German-style 'current particulars' printout — the folio certifies the inscribed instruments rather than a synthesised current state.

evidence.basis: LGSM art. 2
evidence.quote: Las sociedades mercantiles inscritas en el Registro Público de Comercio, tienen personalidad jurídica distinta de la de los socios.
evidence.note: Inscription in the RPC is the constitutive registry act.

evidence.basis: Código de Comercio arts. 20-32 (folio mercantil electrónico / SIGER)
evidence.note: UNVERIFIED verbatim — could not fetch the Código de Comercio text; the folio mercantil electrónico, SIGER and boleta/certificación model is asserted from domain knowledge, not a fetched quote.

summary: Only a SOLE shareholder is registered. A multi-shareholder BV publishes nothing about its shareholders.

evidence.basis: art. 22 lid 1 sub e Handelsregisterbesluit 2008
evidence.note: Requires registration of personal data (excluding signature) of 'de houder van alle aandelen' of an NV or BV — i.e. the sole shareholder only. With two or more shareholders, nothing is registered.

evidence.basis: KVK — uittreksel
evidence.quote: In een uittreksel zie je bijvoorbeeld wie eigenaar, enig aandeelhouder, bestuurder of gevolmachtigde is van een bedrijf.

evidence.basis: art. 2:85 BW — aandeelhoudersregister
evidence.quote: Niet iedereen mag het aandeelhoudersregister inzien
evidence.note: Internal register kept by the board at the company address. Inspection limited to shareholders, pledgees, usufructuaries and certificate holders with meeting rights.

caveats: Partial fallback: the oprichtingsakte/statuten deposited with KvK is orderable by anyone (online only if deposited after 1 June 2013) and names founders + shares at incorporation — a snapshot, never updated for transfers.
UNVERIFIED: KvK screens deposited documents for sensitive information; whether the founder section always survives redaction is unconfirmed.
The planned Centraal Aandeelhoudersregister (CAHR) would also NOT be public.
UBO register closed to the public since CJEU WM/Sovim.

documentLabel: Names of participants (shown on the register extract)
summary: Unlike an AS/ASA, the OWNERS of an ANS — the participants (deltakere) with personal liability — are registered in Foretaksregisteret and MUST appear on the firmaattest. So current owners are evidenced by the base register document itself.

evidence.basis: Forskrift om registrering av foretak §13 (firmaattestens innhold), Lovdata
evidence.quote: Navn på deltakerne i ansvarlig selskap og EØFG.
evidence.note: The firmaattest for an ansvarlig selskap must state the names of the participants (deltakere).

caveats: This evidences the personally-liable participants (deltakere), the ANS analogue of shareholders — a genuine distinction from AS/ASA. Ownership percentages/interest splits may not be fully detailed on the firmaattest; the selskapsavtale gives the detailed shares.

summary: There is NO shareholder-evidencing document issued by the commercial register. Brønnøysundregistrene holds no shareholder data. The aksjeeierbok (shareholder register, aksjeloven §4-5) is kept by the company itself and is open to inspection at the company (§4-6), but it is not a registry-issued document. Separately, shareholder data is reported annually to Skatteetaten's aksjonærregisteret; anyone may request an extract, but it is delivered as a caveated .csv data file (available only in May of the following year), not an authoritative certified document.

evidence.basis: Brønnøysundregistrene – The shareholder register
evidence.quote: The Brønnøysund Register Centre does not keep any record of shareholders.

evidence.basis: Aksjeloven §4-5 / §4-6 (Lovdata)
evidence.note: §4-5: the board must keep an updated aksjeeierbok; §4-6: anyone has the right of inspection into the aksjeeierbok — but this is inspection AT THE COMPANY, not a registry document.

evidence.basis: Skatteetaten – Aksjonærregisteret (deling)
evidence.quote: Alle kan få tilgang til opplysninger om aksjeeiere i aksjonærregisteret
evidence.note: Public access exists but delivery is a downloadable .csv data extract, not a certified document; opens ~May of the year after the tax year.

evidence.basis: Skatteetaten – Aksjonærregisteret (deling)

evidence.note: Skatteetaten itself flags the data as possibly erroneous/incomplete — not authoritative proof.

caveats: CONFLICT of concepts: distinguish (a) the company-kept aksjeeierbok — a real document but held by the company, inspectable under §4-6, NOT obtainable from any registry; from (b) the tax aksjonærregisteret — publicly accessible but delivered as raw .csv DATA that Skatteetaten warns may be wrong/incomplete and is ~4 months stale. Neither is a commercial-register proof document.
INFERENCE: framed strictly as 'a document is the proof, parsed data is not a substitute', the honest status is not_provable_from_registry.

documentLocal: aksjeeierregister i verdipapirsentral (Euronext Securities Oslo/VPS) / aksjonærregisteret (Skatteetaten)

Shareholder data is also reported to Skatteetaten's aksjonærregisteret and obtainable as a caveated .csv extract — data, not a certified document.
